chief_complaint: 43-year-old female with type 2 diabetes mellitus presents with severe diabetic ketoacidosis.
history: Patient self-administered liraglutide at 10 times the recommended dose (cumulative 18 mg over 3 days) for weight loss. Type 2 diabetes mellitus is a major global health concern. Liraglutide, a glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist, is widely used for glycemic control and weight loss.
exam: Vital signs: pH 6.9, beta-hydroxybutyrate markedly elevated, hyperkalemia. Physical examination findings: end-organ compromise.
labs: ECG: not mentioned, Bloods: pH 6.9, beta-hydroxybutyrate markedly elevated, hyperkalemia, Imaging: not mentioned, Other tests: not mentioned.
correct_diagnosis: Diabetic ketoacidosis induced by liraglutide overdose
differentials: Diabetic ketoacidosis induced by liraglutide overdose
correct_ranking: Diabetic ketoacidosis induced by liraglutide overdose
